title: The One Where Rachel's Sister Babysits
weakest_section: 21:00-22:00 range with proposal scene having fewer laughs

jokes.text: And for the record, I've also never given her frosting from a can.
jokes.setup: Ross defensively claiming he's never given Emma a cookie
jokes.punchline: Escalates to the oddly specific denial about canned frosting, implying he has
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross neurotic specificity - the 'for the record' formality paired with canned frosting denial is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's just that we don't think of you as really being so much with the words.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler explaining why they didn't ask Joey to write a recommendation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate way of saying Joey isn't articulate, using unnecessarily complex phrasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler linguistic awkwardness - ironically being verbose while criticizing Joey's word skills

jokes.text: [BABBLING] Clearly, we were wrong.
jokes.setup: Chandler just said Joey isn't good with words
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately proves the point by babbling incoherently, followed by Chandler's dry observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-payoff timing with Joey unwittingly proving Chandler's point immediately

jokes.text: Dear Baby Adoption Decider People...
jokes.setup: Joey attempting to write a formal recommendation letter
jokes.punchline: His hilariously informal and childlike opening to what should be a professional letter
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - his childlike approach to formal situations perfectly captured in one absurd phrase

jokes.text: and we're gonna have sex in a public restroom.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining she's dressing fancy for her anniversary regardless of venue
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions public restroom sex as if it's a normal romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe overshare delivered with complete sincerity, creating perfect cringe comedy

jokes.text: That's where people make number two.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions public restroom sex, Monica reveals Chandler won't even use their bathroom
jokes.punchline: Chandler's childish euphemism for his bathroom phobia
jokes.explanation: Chandler's prissy nature perfectly captured in his juvenile phrasing about bodily functions

jokes.text: You gave a B to a Pottery Barn catalog.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he needs to grade papers instead of having sex
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals he's grading a furniture catalog, exposing his priorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal that undercuts Ross's excuse while showing his academic pretentiousness

jokes.text: Still sharp as a tack.
jokes.setup: Amy says 'You're not Rachel' to Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically compliments her obvious observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross sarcasm in response to Amy's ditzy observation

jokes.text: Did I buy a falafel from you yesterday?
jokes.setup: Ross desperately trying to get Amy to remember him with increasingly personal details
jokes.punchline: Amy thinks he's a random street vendor despite all the context
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating cringe as Amy reduces Ross to a random food vendor after he lists major life events

jokes.text: Yes. Yes, you did.
jokes.setup: Amy asking if Ross sold her falafel
jokes.punchline: Ross giving up and playing along with her completely wrong assumption
jokes.explanation: Ross's defeated acceptance of being mistaken for a food vendor is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: You are not good.
jokes.setup: Joey assuming Amy is a fan because she recognized him from Days of Our Lives
jokes.punchline: Amy bluntly critiquing his acting instead of praising it
jokes.explanation: Amy's brutal honesty delivered in the simplest possible terms deflates Joey's ego perfectly

jokes.text: What do I know? I just sell Middle Eastern food from a cart.
jokes.setup: Amy changes Emma's name and Ross questions it
jokes.punchline: Ross continuing the falafel vendor bit with fake self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the running gag while showing Ross's commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Hey, your English is getting better.
jokes.setup: Ross speaking in the falafel vendor persona
jokes.punchline: Amy condescendingly complimenting his 'improving' English, adding casual racism
jokes.explanation: Amy's oblivious condescension adds another layer to her character's tone-deafness

jokes.text: She may be the hottest girl I've ever hated.
jokes.setup: Joey watching Amy interact with the group
jokes.punchline: His conflicted feelings about Amy summarized in one contradictory statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey line that captures his shallow priorities and honest assessment in one phrase

jokes.text: What did I just say?
jokes.setup: Monica suggests Joey use his thesaurus, Joey says he doesn't know big words
jokes.punchline: Joey not understanding the word 'thesaurus' immediately after claiming ignorance
jokes.explanation: Joey instantly proving his own point about not knowing big words is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Look out, ladies! Joey Tribbiani's got the whole package!
jokes.setup: Joey discovering the thesaurus function and thinking he's now smart
jokes.punchline: His overconfident proclamation using a phrase with sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Joey's premature celebration with unintentional innuendo is classic character comedy

jokes.text: No, I was talking about your bedding.
jokes.setup: Amy says 'so beautiful' while looking at baby Emma, Rachel assumes she means the baby
jokes.punchline: Amy was actually admiring Rachel's sheets, showing her priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that reveals Amy's shallow materialism while deflating Rachel's expectations

jokes.text: And it's on Fifth. And the elevator opens up right into the living room.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking what Amy's fiancé is like
jokes.punchline: Amy only describing the apartment features instead of his personality
jokes.explanation: Amy's materialistic priorities clearly established through her focus on real estate over romance

jokes.text: It's his dad.
jokes.setup: Amy mentions her fiancé, Rachel asks if she remembers her old boyfriend Mark
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's marrying her ex-boyfriend's father
jokes.explanation: Shocking revelation delivered with perfect timing - the awkwardness of the situation is immediately clear

jokes.text: God, we used to make out all the time after you went to sleep.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions her ex-boyfriend Barry as an example
jokes.punchline: Amy casually reveals she used to hook up with Rachel's ex behind her back
jokes.explanation: Amy's casual confession about betraying Rachel shows her complete lack of social awareness

jokes.text: Sometimes just nodding is okay.
jokes.setup: Amy just revealed she made out with Rachel's ex-boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Rachel's restrained advice to stop sharing inappropriate information
jokes.explanation: Rachel's diplomatic way of telling Amy to shut up is perfectly measured and relatable

jokes.text: I'm gonna marry Myron and keep looking for Mr. Right.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving advice about finding true love and not settling
jokes.punchline: Amy completely misses the point and decides to marry for money while cheating
jokes.explanation: Amy's complete misunderstanding of Rachel's heartfelt advice is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's my anniversary.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking a fan to move his nachos from her seat
jokes.punchline: Using her anniversary as justification for demanding better treatment
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's entitled behavior at the game sets up her character arc for the episode

jokes.text: It's our anniversary.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike loudly declaring their love at a basketball game
jokes.punchline: Phoebe announcing to strangers that it's their anniversary as if that explains everything
jokes.explanation: Continuing the running gag but less impactful than the first use

jokes.text: It's the worst way to propose.
jokes.setup: Couple proposing on the big screen at the game
jokes.punchline: Phoebe criticizing the very method Mike was planning to use
jokes.explanation: Sets up the dramatic irony that drives the rest of Phoebe's storyline

jokes.text: It's the longest I ever spent on a computer without looking at porn.
jokes.setup: Joey proudly presenting his recommendation letter
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his typical computer habits while trying to be helpful
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey overshare that reveals more about him than needed while trying to be proud

jokes.text: They are humid, prepossessing Homo sapiens with full-sized aortic pumps.
jokes.setup: Joey used thesaurus on every word of his simple sentence
jokes.punchline: The hilariously overwrought result that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Joey's thesaurus misuse - 'humid' for 'warm' and 'aortic pumps' for 'hearts' is comedy gold

jokes.text: Baby Kangaroo Tribbiani.
jokes.setup: Chandler reading how Joey signed the letter
jokes.punchline: Joey's bizarre signature combining his name with 'Baby Kangaroo'
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to Joey's letter disaster - the signature makes as little sense as the content

jokes.text: Your full-sized aortic pump.
jokes.setup: Chandler telling Joey to write from the heart
jokes.punchline: Using Joey's own ridiculous thesaurus term for 'heart'
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Chandler incorporating Joey's mistake into genuine advice

jokes.text: I'm Erin Brockovich.
jokes.setup: Amy announcing she left Myron and took Rachel's advice
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to a famous activist for making a basic life decision
jokes.explanation: Amy's dramatic self-comparison shows her tendency to make everything about herself

jokes.text: But Erin Brockovich had her own house.
jokes.setup: Amy comparing herself to Erin Brockovich while asking to stay with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel pointing out the obvious flaw in Amy's comparison
jokes.explanation: Rachel's logical response deflates Amy's grandiose comparison perfectly

jokes.text: A moment on the lips, forever on the hips.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why he doesn't want Amy around
jokes.punchline: Repeating Amy's diet advice that hurt his feelings
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's sensitivity about food and his body image in a relatable way

jokes.text: Oh, sweetie, you can't pull this off.
jokes.setup: Amy seeing what Rachel is wearing
jokes.punchline: Bluntly telling Rachel she looks bad in her own clothes
jokes.explanation: Amy's brutal honesty about fashion shows her shallow priorities and lack of tact

jokes.text: I'm curvy and I like it!
jokes.setup: Amy asking if Monica is sure she wants to eat something
jokes.punchline: Monica's defiant response to body shaming
jokes.explanation: Monica's body-positive response is both empowering and funny in context

jokes.text: Word.
jokes.setup: Monica mentioning Chandler wrote her a rap song for their anniversary
jokes.punchline: Chandler confirming it with hip-hop slang
jokes.explanation: Chandler using rap slang is perfectly awkward and in character

jokes.text: Did I just say, 'If I were a guy'?
jokes.setup: Chandler giving advice about male behavior
jokes.punchline: Suddenly realizing he questioned his own gender mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Chandler's self-aware moment about his own masculinity insecurities is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Plus, he'd never suspect it.
jokes.setup: Discussing where Phoebe should propose to Mike
jokes.punchline: Monica suggesting he wouldn't expect a proposal at Foot Locker
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical approach to romance is both logical and absurd

jokes.text: That's interesting, since she died seven years ago.
jokes.setup: Amy saying Nana is on the phone
jokes.punchline: Rachel calmly pointing out their grandmother is dead
jokes.explanation: Rachel's matter-of-fact delivery about death creates perfect dark comedy

jokes.text: I don't think that's what this is.
jokes.setup: Monica saying her throat hurts like the nanny's menstrual cramps
jokes.punchline: Ross's understated response to the obvious biological impossibility
jokes.explanation: Ross's scientific mind gently correcting Monica's illogical connection

jokes.text: Listen, I couldn't help but overhear, because I was trying to.
jokes.setup: Amy joining a private conversation
jokes.punchline: Admitting she was deliberately eavesdropping instead of making an excuse
jokes.explanation: Amy's unexpected honesty about eavesdropping is refreshingly direct and funny

jokes.text: She guessed 8, 9, based on his drawings.
jokes.setup: Monica asking how the adoption agency knew Joey was a child
jokes.punchline: The agency worker estimated Joey's age from his childlike artwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that adds new absurd detail about Joey's childlike letter presentation

jokes.text: Doesn't it make her nose look smaller?
jokes.setup: Rachel horrified that Amy pierced baby Emma's ears
jokes.punchline: Amy focusing on aesthetic benefits instead of acknowledging wrongdoing
jokes.explanation: Amy's shallow focus on appearance while ignoring the violation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Why, did something happen to his falafel cart?
jokes.setup: Rachel warning that Ross will be upset about Emma's ears
jokes.punchline: Amy still thinking Ross is a street vendor despite everything
jokes.explanation: Good persistence of the running gag showing Amy's complete obliviousness

jokes.text: Please tell me those are clip-ons!
jokes.setup: Ross discovering Emma's pierced ears
jokes.punchline: His desperate hope that the earrings aren't permanent
jokes.explanation: Ross's parental panic expressed through desperate optimism is relatable and funny

jokes.text: Monica can be cool and fun at organized indoor projects.
jokes.setup: Amy claiming to be the cool, fun aunt versus Monica
jokes.punchline: Joey's oddly specific and limited endorsement of Monica's coolness
jokes.explanation: Joey's attempt to defend Monica while accidentally insulting her is perfectly written

jokes.text: I am going to be a baby stylist.
jokes.setup: Amy claiming to know what she'll do with her life
jokes.punchline: Her completely made-up career based on one bad decision
jokes.explanation: Amy's instant career invention shows her ability to rationalize any behavior into a life plan

jokes.text: Enter Amy.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying babies don't care if they're slim
jokes.punchline: Amy referring to herself in third person as the solution to this problem
jokes.explanation: Amy's dramatic self-introduction as baby fashion savior is perfectly narcissistic

jokes.text: No more falafel for you!
jokes.setup: Ross furious at Amy for piercing Emma's ears
jokes.punchline: His ultimate threat using the falafel vendor persona
jokes.explanation: Ross finally using Amy's misconception as a weapon is a satisfying payoff to the running gag

jokes.text: Well, if you don't hold it in, you don't get all the nutrients.
jokes.setup: Mike saying he needs to go to the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's completely wrong medical advice about urine retention
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe pseudo-science delivered with complete confidence is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Well, that's not very enlightened.
jokes.setup: Crowd booing her proposal and someone making sexist comment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe lecturing sports fans about gender equality while being booed
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's feminist response to crowd hostility is both admirable and absurdly timed

jokes.text: No, my mommy and daddy aren't home right now.
jokes.setup: Joey answering phone call from adoption agency
jokes.punchline: Playing along with them thinking he's a child
jokes.explanation: Joey commitment to the child persona shows his dedication to helping his friends

jokes.text: Ass and face.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking where Jill gained weight
jokes.punchline: Amy's blunt and unflattering specification
jokes.explanation: Amy's brutally specific gossip shows her mean-spirited nature while bonding with Rachel

jokes.text: She was. Carbs found her.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying Jill was on Atkins diet
jokes.punchline: Amy personifying carbohydrates as hunting down their sister
jokes.explanation: Clever personification of diet failure that's both mean and genuinely witty

jokes.text: Obviously, you have balls.
jokes.setup: Mike being called 'No Balls' by crowd for Phoebe proposing
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal interpretation missing the metaphorical meaning
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's literal mindset creates humor through misunderstanding the insult

jokes.text: Where else would lame Mr. No Balls hide it?
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizes Mike hid the ring in the cake
jokes.punchline: Using the crowd's insult while criticizing the proposal method
jokes.explanation: Phoebe adopting the cruel nickname while being mean about the proposal is darkly funny

jokes.text: There's no one else in the world I would ask to marry me three times.
jokes.setup: Mike proposing to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging their complicated proposal history as a positive
jokes.explanation: Sweet way to turn their proposal failures into a romantic positive

jokes.text: She's gonna be Mrs. No Balls!
jokes.setup: Phoebe accepting Mike's proposal
jokes.punchline: Fan continuing the cruel nickname even during their romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Extends the running gag but feels cruel rather than funny in the romantic context

jokes.text: It's almost as if people don't want to hear that their babies are ugly.
jokes.setup: Amy explaining why her baby styling business isn't going well
jokes.punchline: Her complete misunderstanding of why parents reject her services
jokes.explanation: Amy's oblivious assessment of her business failure is perfectly in character

tmdb_overview: Rachel allows her sister Amy to babysit Emma despite Ross's protests. Joey writes a recommendation letter for Monica and Chandler's adoption, while Phoebe's anniversary surprise gets thwarted.
